---
title: "random_crop"
generated: true
description: "Random-offset fixed-size crop (a pure index slice)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.augment.geometric`](../nitrix-augment-geometric.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:random_crop -->
```python
random_crop(x: Float[Array, '...'], key: Array, *, size: Sequence[int]) -> Float[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:random_crop -->
Random-offset fixed-size crop (a pure index slice).

`size` gives the output extent on **every** axis of `x` (pass an
axis's full length to leave it uncropped).  A uniform random offset
is drawn per axis and the window extracted with `lax.dynamic_slice`
(static size, traced offset -> jit-clean).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:random_crop -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `x` | `Float` | `...` | — | Input tensor of arbitrary rank. |
| `key` | `Array` | — | — | PRNG key. |
| `size` | `Sequence[int]` | — | — | Per-axis output extent, one entry for each axis of `x`. Each entry must not exceed the corresponding axis length; use an axis's full length to leave it uncropped. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...`. The cropped window, of shape `tuple(size)` and the same dtype as `x`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:random_crop -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
